mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals imminent labor market deterioration and falling consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60d returns with > 60% accuracy, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression over 4 years.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side resilience and inventory replenishment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5d returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when filtered to recovery regimes only.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation expectations and anticipated monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.5% MoM are followed by positive 60d returns in > 70% of cases, or the threshold signal shows no predictive edge over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in total nonfarm payrolls signal sustained labor market expansion and rising aggregate income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 5d forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth below 1.0 percent and negative month-over-month retail sales confirm a broad-based demand contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of weak GDP and falling retail sales fails to predict negative 20d returns with > 58% accuracy, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in industrial production combined with housing starts below 1.0 million units signals a deepening manufacturing and construction downturn, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined supply-side weakness fails to predict negative 60d returns during credit crunch periods, or the signal's accuracy drops below 50% in regime-filtered samples.
